Operation 'updateRow' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Description is too minimal. Added clarity about what gets updated (column values), that partial updates are supported, what is returned, and side effects (in-place modification). This helps AI agents understand the operation's scope and behavior.

data.current_description: Updates a row in a Big Table.
data.description_suggestion: Updates column values in a specific row within a Big Table. Accepts partial updates via the UpdateRowRequest schema - only provided column values will be modified. Returns the updated row object with all current column values. Modifies the row in-place without creating a new version.
data.reasoning: Description is too minimal. Added clarity about what gets updated (column values), that partial updates are supported, what is returned, and side effects (in-place modification). This helps AI agents understand the operation's scope and behavior.

message: Operation 'deleteRow' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Description lacks important context about permanence, irreversibility, and side effects. Added details about data loss, return value, and that the row ID becomes invalid. Critical for AI agents to understand the destructive nature of this operation.

data.current_description: Deletes a row from a Big Table.
data.description_suggestion: Permanently removes a row from a Big Table. This operation is irreversible and will delete all column data associated with the row. Returns a success confirmation. The row ID will no longer be valid after deletion.
data.reasoning: Description lacks important context about permanence, irreversibility, and side effects. Added details about data loss, return value, and that the row ID becomes invalid. Critical for AI agents to understand the destructive nature of this operation.

message: Operation 'deleteStash' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_side_effects. Description mentions data deletion but lacks emphasis on permanence and irreversibility. Added clarity about the destructive nature, return value, and that the stash ID becomes invalid. Important for AI agents to understand the consequences.

data.current_description: Deletes a stash and all its data.
data.description_suggestion: Permanently removes a stash and all associated data chunks. This operation is irreversible and cannot be undone. Returns a success confirmation. The stash ID will no longer be valid after deletion.
data.reasoning: Description mentions data deletion but lacks emphasis on permanence and irreversibility. Added clarity about the destructive nature, return value, and that the stash ID becomes invalid. Important for AI agents to understand the consequences.

message: Operation 'setStashData' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Description is vague about what 'chunk' means and lacks details about create vs. update behavior. Added clarity about overwrite semantics, return value, and use case context. Helps AI agents understand when to use this operation and what to expect.

data.current_description: Sets the content of a chunk of data inside a stash.
data.description_suggestion: Stores or overwrites a data chunk within a stash. Accepts data content in the request body. If the chunk already exists, it will be replaced; otherwise, a new chunk is created. Returns the stash object with updated data. Useful for storing temporary data or intermediate computation results.
data.reasoning: Description is vague about what 'chunk' means and lacks details about create vs. update behavior. Added clarity about overwrite semantics, return value, and use case context. Helps AI agents understand when to use this operation and what to expect.

message: Operation 'initiateUpload' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_context. Description lacks details about what information is required, what is returned, and the multi-step workflow. Added context about required inputs, return values (upload ID and URL), session lifecycle, and the relationship to completeUpload. Essential for AI agents to understand the upload workflow.

data.current_description: Initiate a file upload to Glide.
data.description_suggestion: Begins a file upload process by creating an upload session. Requires file metadata (filename, size, type) in the InitiateUploadRequest. Returns an upload ID and temporary upload URL for subsequent file chunk uploads. The upload session remains active until completed or expires. Must be followed by completeUpload to finalize the upload.
data.reasoning: Description lacks details about what information is required, what is returned, and the multi-step workflow. Added context about required inputs, return values (upload ID and URL), session lifecycle, and the relationship to completeUpload. Essential for AI agents to understand the upload workflow.

message: Operation 'completeUpload' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. Description is too minimal and lacks workflow context. Added clarity about prerequisites (initiateUpload must be called first), validation behavior, return value (file reference), and that the upload ID becomes invalid. Helps AI agents understand the multi-step upload workflow and when this operation should be called.

data.description_suggestion: Finalizes a file upload session and makes the uploaded file available in Glide. Must be called after initiateUpload and all file chunks have been uploaded. Validates the complete file and returns the final file reference or URL. After completion, the upload ID is no longer valid.
data.reasoning: Description is too minimal and lacks workflow context. Added clarity about prerequisites (initiateUpload must be called first), validation behavior, return value (file reference), and that the upload ID becomes invalid. Helps AI agents understand the multi-step upload workflow and when this operation should be called.

data.description_suggestion: Creates a new Big Table in the current team. Accepts table configuration including name, columns, and initial data via CreateTableRequest. Returns the created Table object with assigned tableId and metadata. The new table is immediately available for row operations.
data.reasoning: Description is too minimal. Added clarity about what inputs are expected (CreateTableRequest structure), what is returned (Table object with tableId), and side effects (table becomes immediately available). This helps AI agents understand the operation's full scope and consequences.

message: Operation 'replaceTable' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. data.current_description: Replaces the schema and/or data of a Big Table.
data.description_suggestion: Replaces the schema and/or data of an existing Big Table identified by tableId. Accepts ReplaceTableRequest containing updated table configuration and/or row data. Returns the updated Table object. This operation overwrites existing table structure and/or contents - existing data not included in the request may be lost.
data.reasoning: Description lacks critical details about inputs (ReplaceTableRequest), outputs (Table object), and important side effects (data loss risk). Added these details to help AI agents understand the destructive nature of this operation and when it should be used versus update operations.

data.current_summary: Get rows from a Big Table
data.current_description: Gets rows from a Big Table.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves rows from a Big Table identified by tableId. Supports filtering, sorting, and pagination via query parameters. Returns a RowsListResponse containing an array of Row objects and pagination metadata. Useful for fetching subsets of large tables or implementing paginated views.

Added information about the 3 parameters (filtering, sorting, pagination), the response structure (RowsListResponse with metadata), and use cases. message: Operation 'getRowVersion' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, unclear_purpose, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary and description lack clarity about the HEAD method's purpose and use case. Clarified that this is a lightweight metadata check operation, explained what information is returned (headers only), and provided context about when an AI agent should use this (change detection, cache validation) versus full row retrieval.

data.current_description: Retrieve row version information for a Big Table.
data.summary_suggestion: Check Big Table row version metadata
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves version metadata for rows in a Big Table identified by tableId using a HEAD request. Returns version information in response headers without fetching row data, enabling efficient change detection and cache validation. Useful for determining if table contents have been modified since last access.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description lack clarity about the HEAD method's purpose and use case. Clarified that this is a lightweight metadata check operation, explained what information is returned (headers only), and provided context about when an AI agent should use this (change detection, cache validation) versus full row retrieval.
